prompt:
You are a software requirements analyst.
You are given a list of software requirements with their IDs.

Your task is to identify which of the following software requirements are non-functional requirements (NFRs).

Definition:

Functional requirements describe what the system should do, such as features, behaviors, user actions, data operations, or business rules.
Non-functional requirements describe how the system should be, perform, operate, or be constrained. They include quality attributes, system properties, technical constraints, environmental constraints, and operational constraints.

Non-functional requirements include, but are not limited to:

Performance, such as response time, throughput, scalability, capacity, concurrency, or resource usage.
Security, such as access control, authentication, authorization, data protection, integrity, or protection against malicious input.
Maintainability, such as modifiability, configurability, extensibility, code quality, ease of updates, or system evolution.
Usability, such as ease of use, accessibility, learnability, user experience, or user-interface quality expectations.
Reliability, availability, robustness, or fault tolerance.
Compatibility, portability, interoperability, supported platforms, supported environments, or technology constraints.

Important guidelines:

Requirements describing constraints on supported environments, platforms, technologies, compatibility, portability, or interoperability should be considered NFRs.
Requirements about system qualities, system properties, user experience, reliability, availability, or operational constraints should be considered NFRs.
Requirements that only describe a concrete system function, user action, data operation, or business rule should be considered FRs.
If a requirement could reasonably be interpreted as either FR or NFR, classify it based on its primary concern. A behavior, feature, user action, or data operation should be classified as FR. A quality attribute, system property, or explicit constraint should be classified as NFR.
Do not classify a requirement as NFR only because it is vague or difficult to understand.

Instructions:
For each requirement, output exactly one line in the format:
REQ_ID: NFR
or
REQ_ID: FR
Use only the original requirement IDs from the input.
Do not include explanations, summaries, or any extra text.
Do not change the format or add punctuation.

Output format (STRICT):
R1: NFR
R2: FR
R3: NFR
...

Requirements:
1. [R29] The look and feel of the system shall conform to the user interface standards of the smart device.
2. [R30] The user interface shall have standard menus  buttons for navigation
3. [R31] The system shall have a professional appearance
4. [R32] The product shall have a consistent color scheme and fonts.
5. [R33] The system shall be used by realtors with no training.
6. [R34] The system shall be intuitive and self explanatory.
7. [R35] The product shall be easy for a realtor to learn.
8. [R36] The product shall use symbols and words that are naturally understandable by the realtor community.
9. [R37] The product shall produce search results in an acceptable time
10. [R38] The search results shall be returned no later 30      seconds  after the user has entered the search criteria
11. [R39] The product shall generate a CMA report in an acceptable time.
12. [R40] The CMA report shall be returned no later 60 seconds after the user has entered the CMA report criteria.
13. [R41] The product shall synchronize contacts and appointments in an acceptable time.
14. [R42] The product shall synchronize with the office system every hour.
15. [R43] The product shall be available for use 24 hours per day  365 days  per year
16. [R44] Out of 1000 accesses to the system  the system is available 999 times.
17. [R45] The product shall operate in offline mode whenever internet connection is unavailable.
18. [R46] The product shall allow the user to view previously downloaded search results  CMA reports  and appointments.
19. [R47] The product shall retain user preferences in the event of a failure.
20. [R48] 100% of saved user preferences shall be restored when system comes back online.
21. [R49] The product shall be able to support multiple remote users
22. [R50] The product shall be able support 1000 simultaneous users.
23. [R51] The product shall be capable of handling the existing 1000 users. This number is expected to grow 5 times within the next year.
24. [R52] The product shall be installed by an untrained realtor   without recourse to separately-printed instructions.
25. [R53] 90% of untrained realtors shall be able to install the product on their device without printed instructions
26. [R54] The product is expected to run on Windows CE and Palm operating systems.
27. [R55] Only registered realtors shall be able to access the system.
28. [R56] Every user of the system shall be authenticated and authorized.
29. [R57] The product shall prevent its data from incorrect data being introduced.
30. [R58] The system shall allow a real estate agent to query MLS information
31. [R59] The user shall be able to get driving directions to a selected property
32. [R60] The user shall be able to download appointments and contact information for clients
33. [R61] The system shall display clear property images in the search results
34. [R62] The system shall update or create new property listings in the MLS
35. [R63] Realtor shall be able to generate a CMA report based on property criteria
36. [R64] The system shall be able to call the seller or buyer to schedule an appointment
37. [R65] The realtor shall be notified of new client appointments after automatic synchronization with office system
38. [R66] The realtor shall be able to retrieve a map showing property locations for a specified area
39. [R67] The system shall notify the realtor when a seller or buyer responds to an appointment request
40. [R68] The product shall allow the user to save the property search results.
